track.title: Treasury Bills and Bonds
track.description: A beginner route for Kenyan government securities, auctions, DhowCSD, maturity, coupons, and price risk.

course.title: Treasury Bills and Bonds: Core route
course.description: Government securities education before applying through official routes.

lessons.title: What is a Treasury Bill?
lessons.summary: A T-bill is a short-term government security usually bought at a discount and repaid at face value.
lessons.introduction: A Treasury bill is a short-term security issued by the Government of Kenya through the Central Bank of Kenya. It is usually bought below face value and repaid at face value at maturity.
sections: The difference between purchase price and face value is part of the return calculation.
T-bills have defined maturity periods, so timing matters.
Application, payment, auction results, and maturity instructions should be verified through CBK or DhowCSD.

scenario.title: KES 50,000 practice
scenario.text: A user wants to try a T-bill with KES 50,000 but may need the money in six weeks. They check maturity before applying.

lessons.mistake: Using money needed before maturity without understanding the exit route.
lessons.action: Write the face value, estimated purchase price, auction date, and maturity date before applying externally.
lessons.takeaway: A T-bill decision is about timing and mechanics, not only the rate.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify current minimums, auction dates, and application instructions on CBK/DhowCSD.

lessons.title: 91-day, 182-day, and 364-day T-bills
lessons.summary: Different T-bill tenors tie money up for different periods.
lessons.introduction: Kenyan T-bills are commonly discussed by tenor, such as 91-day, 182-day, and 364-day bills. The tenor affects when money is expected back and how the auction result is interpreted.
sections: Shorter tenors may fit nearer cash needs better than longer tenors, but rates and auction outcomes can differ.
Longer tenors keep money committed for more time and should match the user's cash-flow plan.
A tenor is not a guarantee of suitability; it must match the goal and emergency needs.

scenario.title: School fees timing
scenario.text: A parent considering a 182-day bill checks whether school fees are due before maturity.

lessons.mistake: Choosing a tenor because the number sounds better without checking the calendar.
lessons.action: Match each possible tenor to your actual date when the money may be needed.
lessons.takeaway: The right tenor for learning is the one whose timing you understand.
lessons.source_note: Source note: CBK auction and tenor details should be verified before applying.

lessons.title: What is a Treasury Bond?
lessons.summary: A bond is a longer-term government security with maturity and usually coupon payments.
lessons.introduction: A Treasury bond is a longer-term government security. Many bonds pay coupon interest on scheduled dates and repay principal at maturity, but the investor still needs to understand price, timing, and exit risk.
sections: Bonds usually involve longer time horizons than T-bills.
Coupon dates and maturity dates should be written down before deciding.
If sold before maturity, market price can differ from the amount originally paid.

scenario.title: Longer goal example
scenario.text: A user thinking about a multi-year goal compares whether a bond's coupon schedule fits their cash-flow needs.

lessons.mistake: Treating a bond like a short-term savings product when the goal needs flexible access.
lessons.action: Write coupon dates, maturity date, and what happens if you need money early.
lessons.takeaway: Bonds require patience and price-risk awareness.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify bond terms with CBK/DhowCSD and licensed professionals where needed.

lessons.title: Coupon, yield, maturity, and price risk
lessons.summary: Bond language matters because each word affects the decision differently.
lessons.introduction: Coupon, yield, maturity, and price risk are related but not the same. Understanding the words helps prevent confusion between cash payments, return measures, timing, and resale value.
sections: Coupon is the scheduled interest payment under the bond terms.
Yield is a return measure that can change with price and market conditions.
Price risk matters if you may sell before maturity because market prices can move.

scenario.title: Selling early
scenario.text: A user buys a bond but needs cash before maturity. They learn that selling early can expose them to market price movement.

lessons.mistake: Confusing coupon with total return or assuming maturity does not matter.
lessons.action: Define coupon, yield, maturity, and price risk in your own words before comparing bonds.
lessons.takeaway: Bond terms are a map; do not travel without reading it.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify current bond terms and auction information with official CBK/DhowCSD pages.

lessons.title: What is DhowCSD?
lessons.summary: DhowCSD is the official CBK portal for government securities account access and services.
lessons.introduction: DhowCSD is the Central Bank of Kenya's investor portal for government securities services. It is part of the official route users should understand before applying for Treasury bills or bonds.
sections: Users should verify account-opening, application, payment, and portfolio instructions directly from CBK/DhowCSD.
Do not rely on unofficial agents who cannot be verified through official channels.
Keep login details private and never share OTPs, passwords, or PINs with anyone.

scenario.title: Fake helper
scenario.text: A person offers to apply for a T-bill for a fee. The user checks the official DhowCSD route instead of sharing credentials.

lessons.mistake: Letting someone else control an account or credentials because the process feels unfamiliar.
lessons.action: Open official CBK/DhowCSD pages and list the steps before asking for help.
lessons.takeaway: Official route literacy protects both money and credentials.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify current DhowCSD instructions on the official portal.

lessons.title: Treasury bill simulator practice
lessons.summary: Use the simulator to practice face value, discount, purchase price, and maturity timing.
lessons.introduction: The T-bill simulator helps users see the relationship between face value, estimated purchase price, discount interest, and maturity. It is a learning estimate, not an auction result.
sections: Change the rate assumption and notice how purchase price changes.
Change the tenor and notice how timing changes.
Write what the simulator cannot know, such as actual auction outcome and personal cash emergencies.

scenario.title: KES 50,000 estimate
scenario.text: A user tests a KES 50,000 face value and compares how assumptions change the estimated purchase price.

lessons.mistake: Treating the simulator as a CBK result or instruction.
lessons.action: Run one T-bill simulation and write the assumption you would verify with CBK.
lessons.takeaway: Simulation is practice for understanding, not permission to act.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Confirm actual auction results and requirements using CBK/DhowCSD.

quiz_questions.explanation: Coupon refers to scheduled interest under bond terms.

options: It affects when money is expected back
quiz_questions.correct_answer: It affects when money is expected back
quiz_questions.explanation: Timing must match the user's cash needs.

quiz_questions.prompt: What should never be shared with an unofficial helper?

flashcards.back: The amount due at maturity before applicable deductions or charges.
flashcards.example: A bill can be bought below face value.

flashcards.back: The length of time until maturity.
A longer-term government security, often with coupon payments.
flashcards.example: A bond may fit a longer goal better than emergency money.

flashcards.back: The risk that sale price changes if you exit before maturity.
flashcards.example: Selling a bond early can produce a different result.

flashcards.back: CBK's official government securities investor portal.
flashcards.example: Verify T-bill instructions on DhowCSD or CBK.

resources.title: Treasury bill starter checklist
resources.body: A starter checklist for understanding T-bill face value, purchase price, tenor, auction dates, DhowCSD route, payment instructions, and maturity before applying externally.
items: Write auction date and maturity date.
Separate face value from purchase price.
Avoid using money needed before maturity.
